title: quercus brutia subsp. haas
intro: Quercus brutia subsp. haas, also known as the Eastern Turkey Oak, is a subspecies of oak tree native to the eastern parts of Turkey. It is a deciduous tree with medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, leaves, and acorns, which contain flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Quercus brutia subsp. haas are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Quercus brutia subsp. haas is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to support overall health and well-being.
